findings.description: The code example in s1 shows only the `Caused by` portion of the stack trace, omitting the outer exception and its frames. The source contains: `java.lang.RuntimeException: unexpected exception occurred. target entity=[nablarch.sample.ss11.entity.SystemAccountEntity] property=[failedCount] parameter=[...]` followed by `SingleValidationTester.invokeValidation` and `SingleValidationTester.testSingleValidation` frames, and the `ValidationManager.validateAndConvertProperty` frame under the `Caused by` block. A user encountering this error in their test output would first see the `RuntimeException` message, not the `IllegalArgumentException`, so omitting the outer exception makes the error example unrecognizable.
findings.source_evidence:
java.lang.RuntimeException: unexpected exception occurred. target entity=[nablarch.sample.ss11.entity.SystemAccountEntity] property=[failedCount] parameter=[[Ljava.lang.String;@1de891b]
    at nablarch.test.core.entity.SingleValidationTester.invokeValidation(SingleValidationTester.java:122)
    at nablarch.test.core.entity.SingleValidationTester.testSingleValidation(SingleValidationTester.java:70)
...Caused by: ...
    at nablarch.core.validation.ValidationManager.validateAndConvertProperty(ValidationManager.java:377)

findings.description: `RuntimeException` is a PascalCase class appearing in the source code example but is absent from the hints array.
